role_summary: Civil rights movement participant; in this corpus's embedding his testimony pairs with the Highlander Folk School music-tradition cohort and the SCLC Educational Director cluster.
ai_reading: Bates's top semantic neighbor is the joint Candie and Guy Carawan interview (entry 11, count 119), the Highlander Folk School music teachers; his second is Dorothy Foreman Cotton (entry 29, count 111), SCLC's Educational Director. The combination is unusual, the embedding pulling his testimony as much toward the Highlander music tradition as toward SCLC's literacy-and-citizenship pedagogical work. His other neighbors include Julius W. Becton Jr. (entry 71, military-then-FEMA-then-Prairie-View), C. T. Vivian (entry 134, SCLC), and Charles McLaurin (entry 17). His axis positions are all extreme centrist, all within +/-0.07 of zero, the catalog's most-narrow centrist band documented so far (narrower than Hayling's +/-0.18 and McDew's +/-0.15).
biographical_paragraph: Scott Bates's oral history is held at the Library of Congress as entry 116 of the Civil Rights History Project (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. Independent biographical sourcing is pending; the strongest signal currently available for movement role is the embedding pattern visible in the AI's reading above.

sources.title: Scott Bates oral history
